question: Vukic vs. Jodar: Match O/U 23.5
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Aleksandar Vukic and Rafael Jodar in the Miami Open, scheduled for March 20 2026.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed across all sets equals or exceeds 24. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
All tiebreaks—including any Champions/Super tiebreak—count as one (1) game toward the total.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official ATP statistics.
groupItemTitle: Miami Open: Aleksandar Vukic vs Rafael Jodar Match O/U 23.5

events.title: Miami Open: Aleksandar Vukic vs Rafael Jodar
events.description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Aleksandar Vukic and Rafael Jodar in the Miami Open, scheduled for March 20 at 7:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Aleksandar Vukic' if Aleksandar Vukic advances against Rafael Jodar.

This market will resolve to 'Rafael Jodar' if Rafael Jodar advances against Aleksandar Vukic.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Rafael Jodar at 80% implied probability in his Miami Open qualifying clash with Aleksandar Vukic, driven primarily by Vukic's shaky recent hardcourt form—including a first-round exit at Indian Wells to Alexandre Muller and prior qualifying struggles—contrasting Jodar's momentum from a decisive 6-3, 6-2 Q1 win over Murkel Dellien. Vukic holds a significant ranking edge (No. 92 vs. Jodar's No. 477), but no head-to-head exists, and neither reports injuries per official ATP updates. Outdoor hardcourt conditions suit both, though Jodar's youth and qualifier rest advantage amplify upset potential amid Vukic's 4-6 2024 record, shaping crowd wisdom toward the Spaniard.

question: Solana Up or Down - March 22, 1AM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the SOL/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the SOL/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/SOL_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance SOL/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

No injuries reported for either; head-to-head is 0-0, but Huesler's 70% win rate on hard courts this year versus Zink's sub-50% underscores trader consensus on the matchup dynamics, tempered by Zink's home-continent wildcard momentum.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a dead-even matchup at 50% implied probability for Franco Roncadelli against Joao Lucas da Silva in the Asuncion Challenger on clay, driven by their similar ATP rankings around 450-550 and strong regional clay court pedigrees as South American grinders. Roncadelli enters with momentum from a recent semifinal run in M15 Cordoba, showcasing baseline resilience, while da Silva counters with a title win last week in M25 Sao Paulo, highlighting his improving serve hold percentage. No head-to-head history adds uncertainty; odds could shift on pre-match weather delays or confirmed lineups, with Roncadelli's home-country edge potentially tipping if da Silva shows travel fatigue.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a dead-even matchup at 50% implied probability for Franco Roncadelli against Joao Lucas da Silva in the Asuncion Challenger on clay, driven by their similar ATP rankings around 450-550 and strong regional clay court pedigrees as South American grinders. Roncadelli enters with momentum from a recent semifinal run in M15 Cordoba, showcasing baseline resilience, while da Silva counters with a title win last week in M25 Sao Paulo, highlighting his improving serve hold percentage. No head-to-head history adds uncertainty; odds could shift on pre-match weather delays or confirmed lineups, with Roncadelli's home-country edge potentially tipping if da Silva shows travel fatigue.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus tilts slightly toward Kamilla Rakhimova at 52.5% implied probability against Marta Kostyuk in the Miami Open, primarily fueled by Rakhimova's scorching hardcourt form, including a stunning upset over former No. 4 Belinda Bencic in the prior round after winning seven of her last eight matches. Kostyuk, ranked 26th to Rakhimova's 102nd, holds a 2-1 head-to-head edge but arrives off a straight-sets win over Harriet Dart amid a 4-2 recent record marred by early exits elsewhere. The competitive balance stems from both players' solid serving and baseline games suiting the medium-fast courts, with no reported injuries; odds could shift on pre-match warmups revealing fatigue in Kostyuk or Rakhimova's momentum cooling.

Odds could shift on pre-match injury reports—Keys' past hip issues—or serve efficiency, with Ruse thriving if Keys' first-serve percentage dips below 65%. Home-crowd boost for American Keys adds marginal edge.

Momentum could shift if McNally exploits home conditions early or Wang falters from a compressed schedule, underscoring the volatility of early WTA 1000 encounters.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Qinwen Zheng at even money against Sloane Stephens in the Miami Open, reflecting a razor-thin edge for the seventh-seeded Chinese star's baseline power and recent hardcourt dominance, balanced by Stephens' veteran savvy, Miami home-crowd boost, and upset potential as a former major champion. Zheng's Olympic gold pedigree and 75% win rate this season contrast Stephens' streaky form after qualifying triumphs, but their head-to-head void and similar serve efficiencies create parity on these fast courts. No confirmed injuries tilt the scales yet; watch pre-match warmups or weather delays for momentum shifts, as Stephens thrives in gritty three-setters while Zheng imposes aces in straight sets.

question: XRP Up or Down - March 22, 1AM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the XRP/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the XRP/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/XRP_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance XRP/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

eventMetadata.context_description: Elina Svitolina holds a narrow 51% implied probability edge over qualifier Emerson Jones in their Miami Open second-round clash, reflecting trader caution amid Svitolina's veteran pedigree clashing with the 20-year-old Australian's blistering hard-court form. Svitolina, ranked No. 13 with a 14-4 record this season including a Dubai final, boasts superior experience on Miami's medium-fast courts and a perfect 3-0 second-round record here, but recent three-setters signal potential fatigue. Jones, surging after straight-set qualifier wins over Nao Hibino and Moyuka Uchijima, brings fearless momentum and sharp serving that has traders pricing upset potential. A clean Svitolina warmup or Jones nerves could swing odds decisively.

This market refers on the tennis match between Elina Svitolina and Emerson Jones in the Miami Open, scheduled for March 20 at 2:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Elina Svitolina' if Elina Svitolina advances against Emerson Jones.

This market will resolve to 'Emerson Jones' if Emerson Jones advances against Elina Svitolina.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Elina Svitolina holds a narrow 51% implied probability edge over qualifier Emerson Jones in their Miami Open second-round clash, reflecting trader caution amid Svitolina's veteran pedigree clashing with the 20-year-old Australian's blistering hard-court form. Svitolina, ranked No. 13 with a 14-4 record this season including a Dubai final, boasts superior experience on Miami's medium-fast courts and a perfect 3-0 second-round record here, but recent three-setters signal potential fatigue. Jones, surging after straight-set qualifier wins over Nao Hibino and Moyuka Uchijima, brings fearless momentum and sharp serving that has traders pricing upset potential. A clean Svitolina warmup or Jones nerves could swing odds decisively.

question: Svitolina vs. Jones: Match O/U 21.5
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Elina Svitolina and Emerson Jones in the Miami Open, scheduled for March 20 2026.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed across all sets equals or exceeds 22. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
All tiebreaks—including any Champions/Super tiebreak—count as one (1) game toward the total.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official WTA statistics.

eventMetadata.context_description: Flavio Cobolli holds a slim 52.5% implied probability edge over Raphaël Collignon in this Miami Open qualifying clash, driven by his No. 57 ATP ranking and steadier hard-court results against top competition. Collignon's upset win over No. 128 Gabriel Diallo (6-4, 3-6, 6-3) in the prior round has fueled trader balance, showcasing the 21-year-old Frenchman's aggressive baseline game and qualifier momentum on these fast Miami courts. With no head-to-head and both players young (22 for Cobolli), the matchup hinges on Cobolli's travel fatigue from Acapulco versus Collignon's rest advantage—early service holds or break-point conversions could swiftly shift odds amid unpredictable early-round volatility.

question: Dogecoin Up or Down - March 22, 1AM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the DOGE/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the DOGE/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/DOGE_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance DOGE/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

No significant injuries mar Illinois' official report, unlike VCU's nagging frontcourt tweaks. Matchup dynamics favor the Illini historically on neutral courts, with better free-throw rates and transition pace amplifying trader consensus on their dominance.

question: Jalen Brunson: Points O/U 24.5
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for March 20 at 7:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Jalen Brunson scores more than 24.5 points during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if Jalen Brunson scores 24.5 points or fewer during the game.

The entire game including all overtime periods will be considered.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not take the court at any point, the market will resolve "No".

The resolution source will be the official NBA box score as published on NBA.com.
groupItemTitle: Jalen Brunson: Points O/U 24.5

events.title: Knicks vs. Nets
events.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for March 20 at 7:30PM ET:
If the Knicks win, the market will resolve to "Knicks".
If the Nets win, the market will resolve to "Nets".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: New York Knicks hold an 88.5% implied probability as heavy favorites against the Brooklyn Nets, driven primarily by their superior recent form and healthier lineup. The Knicks boast a 5-2 record early in the season, fueled by Jalen Brunson's 28.6 points per game and Karl-Anthony Towns' return to full strength after a preseason ankle tweak, per official injury reports. In contrast, the Nets sit at 1-6, hampered by Nic Claxton's extended knee absence and Cam Thomas' questionable status with a back issue, weakening their defense and scoring. Knicks also own a 4-1 edge in recent head-to-heads, amplified by home-court advantage at Madison Square Garden, where they shoot 48% from the field. Trader consensus reflects these roster and momentum edges, though Nets' desperation could spark an upset.

question: Karl-Anthony Towns: Points O/U 19.5
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for March 20 at 7:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Karl-Anthony Towns scores more than 19.5 points during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if Karl-Anthony Towns scores 19.5 points or fewer during the game.

The entire game including all overtime periods will be considered.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not take the court at any point, the market will resolve "No".

The resolution source will be the official NBA box score as published on NBA.com.
groupItemTitle: Karl-Anthony Towns: Points O/U 19.5

events.title: Knicks vs. Nets
events.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for March 20 at 7:30PM ET:
If the Knicks win, the market will resolve to "Knicks".
If the Nets win, the market will resolve to "Nets".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: New York Knicks hold an 88.5% implied probability as heavy favorites against the Brooklyn Nets, driven primarily by their superior recent form and healthier lineup. The Knicks boast a 5-2 record early in the season, fueled by Jalen Brunson's 28.6 points per game and Karl-Anthony Towns' return to full strength after a preseason ankle tweak, per official injury reports. In contrast, the Nets sit at 1-6, hampered by Nic Claxton's extended knee absence and Cam Thomas' questionable status with a back issue, weakening their defense and scoring. Knicks also own a 4-1 edge in recent head-to-heads, amplified by home-court advantage at Madison Square Garden, where they shoot 48% from the field. Trader consensus reflects these roster and momentum edges, though Nets' desperation could spark an upset.

In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for March 20 at 8:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Julius Randle records more than 4.5 assists during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if Julius Randle records 4.5 assists or fewer during the game.

The entire game including all overtime periods will be considered.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not take the court at any point, the market will resolve "No".

The resolution source will be the official NBA box score as published on NBA.com.
groupItemTitle: Julius Randle: Assists O/U 4.5

events.title: Trail Blazers vs. Timberwolves
events.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for March 20 at 8:00PM ET:
If the Trail Blazers win, the market will resolve to "Trail Blazers".
If the Timberwolves win, the market will resolve to "Timberwolves".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: Timberwolves' six-game win streak and Portland's mounting injuries anchor the 72% implied probability favoring Minnesota, reflecting trader consensus on the Wolves' defensive dominance and offensive firepower led by Anthony Edwards. The Trail Blazers, mired in a five-game skid with a 2-8 record, face a setback without Jerami Grant (out, knee) and Deandre Ayton (questionable, illness), crippling their frontcourt rebounding against Minnesota's elite paint protection. Head-to-head, the Wolves have won seven of the last 10 meetings, bolstered by home-court edge and fresher legs off two days' rest versus Portland's back-to-back fatigue. While upsets occur, recent form and roster health heavily tilt market sentiment toward Minnesota.

question: Raptors vs. Nuggets: O/U 235.5
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for March 20 at 9:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the Raptors and Nuggets combine to score 236 or more points in this game.

If the combined total is less than 236, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

events.title: Raptors vs. Nuggets
events.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for March 20 at 9:00PM ET:
If the Raptors win, the market will resolve to "Raptors".
If the Nuggets win, the market will resolve to "Nuggets".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: Denver Nuggets' strong home-court advantage and recent surge—winning four of their last five games—anchor the 74% implied probability against a slumping Toronto Raptors squad on a three-game skid with a 2-6 record. Nikola Jokic's dominance (30+ points, double-digit rebounds lately) exploits Toronto's thin frontcourt, especially with Jakob Poeltl questionable (ankle) and no reliable counter. Jamal Murray is fully cleared for Denver, while Raptors' Immanuel Quickley remains sidelined (patellar tendon). Head-to-head, Nuggets have taken six of the last seven meetings, underscoring matchup edges in rebounding and pace control that trader consensus reflects in current pricing.

question: Bitcoin Up or Down - March 21, 1:05AM-1:10AM ET
description:

The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the BTC/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/btc-usd.

This market will resolve to "Up" if the Bitcoin price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the BTC/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/btc-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream BTC/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the SOL/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/sol-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream SOL/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.
